output.output:
# hello-world-api translation

Translate the Flask source API in `src/` into a Java Spring Boot API in `dst/`.

The Java service must be a drop-in replacement:

- Listen on the port from the `SERVER_PORT` environment variable.
- Serve `GET /`.
- Return JSON with a `message` field.
- Without `name`, return `{"message":"Hello World!"}`.
- With `name`, trim leading/trailing spaces and return `{"message":"Hello <name>!"}`.

Put all target implementation files under `dst/`. The expected build command is:

```sh
mvn clean package -DskipTests
```

The expected run command is:

```sh
SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar
```

text: The repo is minimal: one Flask entrypoint and an empty `dst/`. I’m reading the Python behavior exactly, then I’ll scaffold a Spring Boot app that mirrors it and packages as a runnable jar.

input.title: hello-world-api Spring Boot drop-in implementation
input.content: Implemented the `hello-world-api` replacement service directly in Java under `dst/` as a standalone Spring Boot app. It builds with `mvn clean package -DskipTests` on Java 17 and runs with `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar`. The HTTP behavior matches the Flask source and README: `GET /` returns JSON with a single `message` field, defaults to `{"message":"Hello World!"}`, trims the optional `name` query parameter, and falls back to `World` when the trimmed value is empty.

text:
Implemented the Flask API directly in Spring Boot under `dst/`. The app bootstrap is in [HelloWorldApiApplication.java](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworldapi/HelloWorldApiApplication.java:1), the HTTP behavior is in [HelloController.java](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworldapi/HelloController.java:1), and the Maven build is defined in [pom.xml](/home/user/workspace/dst/pom.xml:1).

The endpoint behavior matches the README: `GET /` returns `{"message":"Hello World!"}` by default, trims `name`, returns `{"message":"Hello <name>!"}` when present, and falls back to `World` for blank trimmed input. `SERVER_PORT` is honored by Spring Boot’s environment binding, so `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar` runs on port 3000 as required.

Verified locally:
- `cd dst && mvn clean package -DskipTests` succeeded
- `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar` started successfully
- `curl` checks returned the expected JSON for `/`, `/?name=Alice`, `/?name=%20%20Bob%20%20`, and `/?name=%20%20%20`

One note: `/home/user/workspace` is not a Git repository here, so I couldn’t provide `git status` output.
